schedule: daily for official calculators; weekly for form libraries unless source changes are detected
steps: Read forms-catalog, form-packet-catalog, and calculators-catalog records from the approved internal source.
Fetch official source page and official file URLs.
Record HTTP status, content length, last-modified header, etag, page hash, and file hash when available.
If URL breaks, source hash changes, PDF hash changes, or effective-date text changes, set status to needs_review.
Create an internal review task and keep public output on official-source fallback until reviewed.
After review, update verified metadata, cached file URL if allowed, and last_verified_at.
Publish source-monitoring-report with public-safe status metadata only.
Classify non-court form-name indexes as reference_index and never promote them to public downloads without an official court source match.
Skip automated monitoring for access-restricted reference sites and keep them out of public route decisions.
Extract Maricopa candidate packet links into a review file before promoting any packet page or download.
Build a form-review-queue file that allows official packet-page actions only when the source is OK and the candidate is the representative packet page.
Extract official PDF candidates from allowed Maricopa packet pages.
Hash PDF candidates and keep direct cached downloads disabled until PDF-level URLs, hashes, and human review are maintained.
Build a PDF review queue grouped by packet and language so approved PDFs can later be promoted without opening unreviewed downloads.
Build public PDF action manifest from explicit human-review decisions; no decision means pending and no public action.
Build a PDF review workbench grouped by packet batch so review decisions can be made, validated, and promoted without exposing unreviewed PDF actions.
Build a reviewer decision template and validate explicit decisions before any official PDF action can be promoted.
Build a dry-run promotion audit that reports promotion-ready, blocked, packet-page-only, rejected, and pending decisions without enabling public PDF actions.
